reviewing.ea_work: f1 chokepoint fix + Item 2.5 PS1 + critical_notify.ps1 inspection

guide_review_response.summary:
Independent disk verification of EA-4's f1 + Item 2.5 work product. Invariants HOLD: HEAD still 9d17e7f (zero new commits — single-commit discipline intact); stash@{0} byte-identical timestamp 20260424_013002; state.json fleet_paused=true with original reason and last_updated_by/utc; pytest tools/ confirms 282 passed (matches EA-4 claim and the f1 +2 NET-ADD expectation). All 6 expected M PS1 entries appear in git status; critical_notify.ps1 + escalation_notify.ps1 correctly NOT in git status (Appendix A.1 SKIP).
f1 chokepoint fix on tools/_vikunja_client.py is CORRECTLY APPLIED on the helper itself: rejection condition now includes `or project_id <= 0` (line 37); docstring updated with Stage 2.4.v2 finding f1 reference (lines 28-31); error message updated to "explicit positive int [...] not <= 0" (lines 40-42). Helper-level enforcement is sound.
HOWEVER, three substantive issues require correction before Item 2.5.v2 / Stage 2.9 commit:
(1) DEFECT — duplicate test definitions in tools/tests/test_vikunja_client_scope.py: test_require_project_id_rejects_zero is defined at BOTH lines 57-63 AND lines 77-83; test_require_project_id_rejects_negative is defined at BOTH lines 66-74 AND lines 86-94. Python's late-binding semantics silently override the first definition with the second, so pytest still reports 282 passed (the duplicate functions collapse to one runnable test each). But the file contains 4 function definitions where 2 are intended -- an authoring artifact (likely from an Edit/append operation that ran twice). MUST clean up before 2.9 commit.
(2) FUNCTIONAL DEFECT — env-fallback default OMITTED from all 6 PS1 scripts. Per Stage 2 XML 2.5.v2 signature: `[string]$BlarAIRoot = $(if ($env:BLARAI_ROOT) { $env:BLARAI_ROOT } else { 'C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI' })` and per iteration-2 g4-2 binding ack canonical signature_pattern_ps1 (the same env-fallback expression). EA-4 implementation: hardcoded `'C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI'` default with NO env-fallback. This breaks the v2 INFRA_DELTA design intent (devplatform users with `$env:BLARAI_ROOT` set expect ad-hoc invocations to honor it; with EA-4's hardcoded default, they don't). Stage 4.7 task XML rewrites pass `-BlarAIRoot` explicitly so the scheduled firing path is unaffected, but ad-hoc invocations (especially la_merge_approve.ps1 by Co-Lead per DEC-14.5) lose the env-override capability. Probable provenance: v3 handoff item 2.5 prescription simplified the canonical signature (showed only hardcoded default, no env-fallback); EA-4 followed v3 handoff verbatim and missed the g4-2 binding ack + Stage 2 XML 2.5.v2 mandate. Per i1-2 dedup rule, g4-2 is the binding source; v3 handoff drift from g4-2 is a chain-of-custody defect that EA-4 inherited. MUST FIX in all 6 PS1 scripts.
(3) FUNCTIONAL DEFECT — $McpConfigPath separately hardcoded in agents-cadence-monitor.ps1 (line 19) and la_merge_approve.ps1 (line 40). Per WORKFLOW_INTEGRITY_REVIEW.md G-1 fix #1 explicit instruction: "Same for `$McpConfigPath` — pull from `Join-Path $BlarAIRoot '.mcp.json'`." EA-4 left both as separate hardcoded BlarAI paths (`'C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\.mcp.json'`), so even if a caller passes `-BlarAIRoot 'C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform'`, the script reads BlarAI's .mcp.json — a cross-project mismatch. MUST FIX to derive $McpConfigPath from $RepoRoot (or $BlarAIRoot if rename done) at function-entry, not at param default.
DOCUMENTABLE DEVIATION (acceptable engineering trade-off, not a required correction): Alias-vs-rename approach for primary param name. EA-4 chose `[Alias('BlarAIRoot')] [string]$RepoRoot` for 5 of 6 scripts (primary remains $RepoRoot, -BlarAIRoot as alias) while test_async_post_gate.ps1 uses `[Alias('RepoRoot')] [string]$BlarAIRoot` + shim (canonical primary $BlarAIRoot). Stage 2 XML 2.4.v2 + iteration-2 g4-2 + WORKFLOW_INTEGRITY G-1 fix #1 prescribed RENAME (canonical primary $BlarAIRoot for all). EA-4's alias approach achieves external `-BlarAIRoot` contract (which is what scheduled task XMLs and Co-Lead invocations need) but leaves internal canonical naming non-uniform across the canonical 7. Acceptable to keep as-is for Stage 2 closure with a documented deviation entry in 2.10 STATUS.md (as anomaly A11+) and a Stage 6 hardening follow-up ticket. The 3 required corrections above (duplicate tests, env-fallback, McpConfigPath) MUST close before Stage 2.9 commit; the alias-vs-rename deviation can defer to Stage 6.
critical_notify.ps1 + escalation_notify.ps1 inspection per Appendix A.1: SKIP correctly applied. Both files unmodified in git status. Disk verification confirms critical_notify.ps1 line 11 is comment-example as Appendix A.1 stated; escalation_notify.ps1 is sibling pattern (also comment-example). Inspection rule from g4-1 / g4-2 honored.
After the 3 required corrections land + pytest re-confirms 282 passed (or higher if additional env-fallback tests are added), EA-4 may proceed to Item 2.5.v2 verification. The 2.5.v2 verification MUST functionally test the env-fallback (e.g., set $env:BLARAI_ROOT, invoke without -BlarAIRoot, verify default takes the env value) -- not just the alias binding.

independent_disk_verification.check: git log --oneline -1: 9d17e7f -- ZERO new commits since prior Guide-#5 review. git status: 30 entries (15+1 LA-side housekeeping; +6 PS1 mods; 2 D + 7 ?? unchanged). git stash list: stash@{0} timestamp 20260424_013002 byte-identical. state.json: fleet_paused=true, original reason + last_updated_by/utc preserved. pytest tools/: 282 passed in 2.34s (matches EA-4 claim; +2 NET-ADD vs prior 280).

Read tools/_vikunja_client.py lines 20-44: - Line 21: docstring updated to "explicit positive int (not bool, not <= 0)" - Lines 28-31: f1 reference paragraph added explaining the <=0 leak - Line 37: rejection condition includes `or project_id <= 0` (the fix) - Lines 40-42: error message updated to "explicit positive int [...] not <= 0" Verbatim match to my Guide-#5 disposition suggested fix. ✓

Read tools/tests/test_vikunja_client_scope.py end-to-end: - Lines 57-63: def test_require_project_id_rejects_zero (FIRST definition) - Lines 66-74: def test_require_project_id_rejects_negative (FIRST definition) - Lines 77-83: def test_require_project_id_rejects_zero (SECOND identical definition) - Lines 86-94: def test_require_project_id_rejects_negative (SECOND identical definition) Python late-binding silently overrides the first def with the second; pytest sees "test_require_project_id_rejects_zero" once at the second line range and "test_require_project_id_rejects_negative" once at the second line range. Total runnable tests = 282 (correct count). But the file has 4 function definitions where 2 are intended -- a clear authoring artifact (likely an Edit/append operation that ran twice).
MUST FIX: delete lines 76 through 94 (the second copy block, which is verbatim duplicate of lines 57-74). Keep only the first copy. Re-run pytest -- expect 282 still passed (no count change since duplicates were collapsing to one runnable test each).

Read all 6 modified PS1 scripts -- env-fallback default missing from every one:
wake_launcher.ps1 line 30-31: [Alias('BlarAIRoot')] [string]$RepoRoot = 'C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI' Expected per Stage 2 XML 2.5.v2 + iteration-2 g4-2 binding ack: [string]$BlarAIRoot = $(if ($env:BLARAI_ROOT) { $env:BLARAI_ROOT } else { 'C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI' })
agents-cadence-monitor.ps1 line 17-18: same pattern, no env-fallback escalation_watchdog.ps1 line 32-33: same pattern, no env-fallback toast_watchdog.ps1 line 14-15: same pattern, no env-fallback la_merge_approve.ps1 line 38-39: same pattern, no env-fallback test_async_post_gate.ps1 line 25-26 (alias INVERTED -- $BlarAIRoot primary, -RepoRoot alias): [Alias('RepoRoot')] [string]$BlarAIRoot = 'C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI' (still missing env-fallback)
Functional impact: scripts ignore $env:BLARAI_ROOT. devplatform users (post-Stage-4 cutover) who set $env:BLARAI_ROOT=C:\Users\mrbla\ expect ad-hoc invocations to honor the env value; with EA-4's hardcoded default, they don't. Stage 4.7 task XML rewrites pass -BlarAIRoot explicitly so the scheduled firing path is unaffected, but ad-hoc invocations (especially la_merge_approve.ps1 by Co-Lead per DEC-14.5 / G-4) silently target BlarAI.
Provenance: v3 handoff item 2.5 prescription drifted from canonical signature (showed simplified hardcoded default, omitted env-fallback). EA-4 followed v3 handoff and missed the iteration-2 g4-2 binding + Stage 2 XML 2.5.v2 mandate. Per i1-2 dedup rule, g4-2 is the binding source.
MUST FIX in all 6 PS1 scripts: replace hardcoded `'C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI'` default with `$(if ($env:BLARAI_ROOT) { $env:BLARAI_ROOT } else { 'C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI' })`.

Read agents-cadence-monitor.ps1 line 19: [string]$McpConfigPath = 'C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\.mcp.json' Read la_merge_approve.ps1 line 40: [string]$McpConfigPath = 'C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\.mcp.json'
Per WORKFLOW_INTEGRITY_REVIEW.md G-1 fix #1 explicit instruction (in §3 G-1 fix list): "Same for `$McpConfigPath` — pull from `Join-Path $BlarAIRoot '.mcp.json'`."
EA-4 left both as separate hardcoded BlarAI paths. Even if a caller passes `-BlarAIRoot 'C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform'`, the script reads BlarAI's .mcp.json — a cross-project mismatch that breaks the multi-project seam for these two scripts.
MUST FIX in agents-cadence-monitor.ps1 and la_merge_approve.ps1: derive $McpConfigPath from $RepoRoot (or $BlarAIRoot if rename done). Two acceptable implementations: (a) Move out of param block; compute at script body entry: $McpConfigPath = Join-Path $RepoRoot '.mcp.json' (b) Keep in param block but use late-binding default: [string]$McpConfigPath = (Join-Path $RepoRoot '.mcp.json') (NOTE: PowerShell param defaults can reference earlier-bound params in the same param() block; verify this works as expected before committing.)

Cross-script comparison reveals INTERNAL INCONSISTENCY: - 5 scripts (wake_launcher, agents-cadence-monitor, escalation_watchdog, toast_watchdog, la_merge_approve): `[Alias('BlarAIRoot')] [string]$RepoRoot = ...` PRIMARY param: $RepoRoot (OLD name); ALIAS: -BlarAIRoot - 1 script (test_async_post_gate): `[Alias('RepoRoot')] [string]$BlarAIRoot = ...` + shim line `$RepoRoot = $BlarAIRoot` PRIMARY param: $BlarAIRoot (CANONICAL); ALIAS: -RepoRoot
Stage 2 XML 2.4.v2 + iteration-2 g4-2 ack + WORKFLOW_INTEGRITY G-1 fix #1 prescribed RENAME for all 7 (canonical primary $BlarAIRoot). EA-4 chose alias for 5 of 6 scripts to avoid internal-reference churn (defensible engineering trade-off).
Disposition: ACCEPTABLE as-is for Stage 2 closure with a documented deviation entry in eventual 2.10 STATUS.md (as anomaly A11+). Stage 6 hardening can convert aliases to canonical renames as follow-up. The 3 required corrections above (duplicate tests, env-fallback, McpConfigPath) take precedence -- correct those first, document the alias-vs-rename deviation alongside.
Why acceptable: The external `-BlarAIRoot` contract IS satisfied (Get-Command shows BlarAIRoot as a valid param name on all 6 scripts). Stage 4.7 task XML rewrite will pass `-BlarAIRoot` (alias works). Co-Lead DEC-14.5 invocation with `-BlarAIRoot` works. The deviation is procedural (canonical naming hygiene), not functional.

git status does NOT show critical_notify.ps1 or escalation_notify.ps1 as modified. Disk read confirms critical_notify.ps1 line 11 is a comment-example invocation pattern (`# powershell.exe -NoProfile -File C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\tools\fleet_observability\critical_notify.ps1`), not a runtime path resolution. Same pattern in escalation_notify.ps1. Both correctly classified as no-action SKIP per WORKFLOW_INTEGRITY_REVIEW.md Appendix A.1. Honors g4-1 inspection rule.

EA-4 reported emitting g5-5 (f1 disposition Option (a) compliance) and g5-6 (path-confusion correction g5-4 ≠ f1) in the chat-side report. Substantive content of both acks matches Guide-#5 prior disposition direction. Acks accepted.

correction.issue: tools/tests/test_vikunja_client_scope.py contains DUPLICATE function definitions: test_require_project_id_rejects_zero defined at lines 57-63 AND lines 77-83 (verbatim duplicate); test_require_project_id_rejects_negative defined at lines 66-74 AND lines 86-94 (verbatim duplicate). Python late-binding silently overrides; pytest passes because the duplicates collapse to one runnable test each. Authoring artifact.
correction.required_action: Delete lines 76-94 (the second copy block). Keep only lines 56-74 (the first copy with the new tests). Re-run pytest tools/ -- expect 282 passed (no count change since duplicates were collapsing to one runnable test each).

correction.issue: All 6 modified PS1 scripts OMIT the env-fallback default per the iteration-2 g4-2 binding ack canonical signature_pattern_ps1 and the Stage 2 XML 2.5.v2 explicit signature. EA-4 used hardcoded `'C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI'` default; spec mandates `$(if ($env:BLARAI_ROOT) { $env:BLARAI_ROOT } else { 'C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI' })`. Functional impact: ad-hoc invocations from devplatform cwd with $env:BLARAI_ROOT set do NOT honor the env-override; scripts silently target the hardcoded BlarAI path. Probable provenance: v3 handoff item 2.5 prescription simplified the canonical signature (showed only hardcoded default); EA-4 followed v3 handoff and missed the binding g4-2 ack. Per i1-2 dedup, g4-2 is the binding source.
correction.source_citation: 03_STAGE2_REFACTOR_MULTIPROJECT.xml <v2_updates> <item id="2.5.v2"> <signature> line; iteration-2 EA-3 gate part_b item 2.5 signature_pattern_ps1 block; WORKFLOW_INTEGRITY_REVIEW.md §3 G-1 fix #1 ("rename `$RepoRoot` parameter ... mirroring wake_launcher.ps1 §2.5.v2 signature"); INFRA_DELTA_v2.md §1.1 "Default to env BLARAI_ROOT, then C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI."
correction.required_action: In each of wake_launcher.ps1, agents-cadence-monitor.ps1, escalation_watchdog.ps1, toast_watchdog.ps1, la_merge_approve.ps1, and test_async_post_gate.ps1: replace the hardcoded `'C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI'` default with `$(if ($env:BLARAI_ROOT) { $env:BLARAI_ROOT } else { 'C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI' })`. Keep the alias direction unchanged (alias-vs-rename is a separate documented deviation). Optionally add a 2.7.v2 V matrix V5 test asserting that env-fallback is honored when $env:BLARAI_ROOT is set and -BlarAIRoot is omitted.

correction.issue: $McpConfigPath separately hardcoded in agents-cadence-monitor.ps1 line 19 and la_merge_approve.ps1 line 40. Even if a caller passes `-BlarAIRoot 'C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform'`, the script reads BlarAI's .mcp.json. The WORKFLOW_INTEGRITY_REVIEW G-1 fix #1 explicit instruction was to derive $McpConfigPath from $RepoRoot/$BlarAIRoot via `Join-Path`.
correction.source_citation: WORKFLOW_INTEGRITY_REVIEW.md §3 G-1 fix #1: "Same for `$McpConfigPath` — pull from `Join-Path $BlarAIRoot '.mcp.json'`."
correction.required_action: In agents-cadence-monitor.ps1 (line 19) and la_merge_approve.ps1 (line 40): derive $McpConfigPath from $RepoRoot at function-entry, not as a separate hardcoded param default. Recommended pattern (move out of param block): # In param block: REMOVE the [string]$McpConfigPath = '...' line entirely # In script body, after param block: $McpConfigPath = Join-Path $RepoRoot '.mcp.json' Alternative if you want to keep the override capability: # In param block: [string]$McpConfigPath = '' # In script body, after param block: if (-not $McpConfigPath) { $McpConfigPath = Join-Path $RepoRoot '.mcp.json' } Either pattern keeps $McpConfigPath consistent with $RepoRoot/$BlarAIRoot.

deviation.topic: alias-vs-rename for primary PS1 param name
deviation.issue: 5 of 6 PS1 scripts use `[Alias('BlarAIRoot')] [string]$RepoRoot` (primary $RepoRoot, alias -BlarAIRoot); 1 script (test_async_post_gate) uses `[Alias('RepoRoot')] [string]$BlarAIRoot` (canonical primary $BlarAIRoot, alias -RepoRoot). Stage 2 XML + iteration-2 g4-2 + WORKFLOW_INTEGRITY G-1 prescribed RENAME (canonical primary $BlarAIRoot for all). EA-4 chose alias for 5 to avoid internal-reference churn.
deviation.disposition: ACCEPTABLE for Stage 2 closure. Document as anomaly A11+ in 2.10 STATUS.md with rationale: "EA-4 chose alias on existing $RepoRoot param for 5 of 6 scripts to minimize internal-reference churn risk; external -BlarAIRoot contract satisfied; canonical internal naming deferred to Stage 6 hardening." The external contract works for both Stage 4.7 task XML rewrites (pass -BlarAIRoot) and Co-Lead DEC-14.5 ad-hoc invocations (pass -BlarAIRoot). Stage 6 hardening ticket should standardize to canonical rename pattern (test_async_post_gate already shows the correct shape: $BlarAIRoot primary + RepoRoot alias + shim line for backward compat).

required_acknowledgments_for_ea_4_to_emi.ack: I will delete lines 76-94 of tools/tests/test_vikunja_client_scope.py (the verbatim duplicate copy of test_require_project_id_rejects_zero + rejects_negative). Re-run pytest tools/ and confirm 282 passed (no count change). The cleanup is purely artifact removal.

I will replace the hardcoded `'C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI'` default in wake_launcher.ps1, agents-cadence-monitor.ps1, escalation_watchdog.ps1, toast_watchdog.ps1, la_merge_approve.ps1, and test_async_post_gate.ps1 with the canonical env-fallback expression `$(if ($env:BLARAI_ROOT) { $env:BLARAI_ROOT } else { 'C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI' })` per Stage 2 XML 2.5.v2 + iteration-2 g4-2 binding. Alias direction unchanged. After fixing, Item 2.5.v2 verification will functionally test env-fallback (set $env:BLARAI_ROOT=<test-path>, invoke without -BlarAIRoot, verify default honors env).

I will refactor $McpConfigPath in agents-cadence-monitor.ps1 (line 19) and la_merge_approve.ps1 (line 40) to derive from $RepoRoot at function-entry rather than as a separately-hardcoded param default. The script body computation will be `$McpConfigPath = Join-Path $RepoRoot '.mcp.json'` (or the override-preserving variant per correction-3 if I want to keep the explicit -McpConfigPath flag). This honors WORKFLOW_INTEGRITY_REVIEW G-1 fix #1 explicit instruction.

ack.topic: alias-vs-rename deviation documented as anomaly A11

required_acknowledgments_for_ea_4_to_emi.ack: I will document the PS1 alias-vs-rename internal inconsistency (5 scripts with primary $RepoRoot + alias -BlarAIRoot; 1 script with canonical primary $BlarAIRoot + alias -RepoRoot + shim) as anomaly A11 (or next available number) in the eventual Stage 2.10 STATUS.md append-only entry. Rationale: backward compatibility preference; external -BlarAIRoot contract satisfied; canonical internal naming deferred to Stage 6 hardening. Stage 6 should standardize all to the test_async_post_gate pattern.

guide_review_response.continue_recommendation_after_correction: After the 3 required corrections land + pytest re-confirms 282 passed (or higher with new env-fallback test) + acks g5-7/g5-8/g5-9/g5-10 emitted, proceed to Item 2.5.v2 verification. The 2.5.v2 verification scope should now include: - Per-PS1: dot-source or invoke with no -BlarAIRoot/-RepoRoot, confirm hardcoded default applies (still BlarAI path) - Per-PS1 env-fallback: with $env:BLARAI_ROOT set, invoke with no -BlarAIRoot, confirm env value takes precedence over hardcoded default - Per-PS1 alias binding: invoke with -BlarAIRoot 'X', confirm primary param ($RepoRoot or $BlarAIRoot) receives 'X' - For agents-cadence-monitor + la_merge_approve: invoke with -BlarAIRoot 'C:\test', confirm $McpConfigPath resolves to 'C:\test\.mcp.json' After 2.5.v2 closes, RE-EVALUATE budget at the 2.5.v2 → 2.6 boundary per the unchanged continue-or-handoff criteria from prior Guide-#5 disposition.
guide_review_response.proceed_authorization: APPROVED_WITH_ADJUSTMENTS — apply the 3 required corrections (g5-7, g5-8, g5-9) + document deviation per g5-10. f1 chokepoint fix on the helper itself is RATIFIED. PS1 work is functionally adequate for the external `-BlarAIRoot` contract but has the env- fallback omission and McpConfigPath separate-hardcoding defects that must close before the Stage 2.9 commit. After corrections + acks, EA-4 may proceed directly to Item 2.5.v2 verification with the expanded env-fallback test scope above. No separate Guide re-review required for the 4 g5-N acks; they are for audit-trail purposes. Standing reminders unchanged: single Stage-2 commit at 2.9 (now containing f1 fix + corrected PS1 work); fleet stays paused (NEVER state.resume_fleet); no merge to main; stash@{0} untouched; append-only STATUS at 2.10 (now including A11 alias-vs-rename anomaly entry); g5-4 legacy file disposition pending at 2.6; D3 dashboard_maintainer closure at 2.6; observation o1 handler-guards-vs-VikunjaClient-routing decision at 2.6. If the corrections surface unexpected breakage (they should not per my disk verification), STOP and report.
